title: Coconut Ladoo

subs.name: desiccated coconut — use 1.5 cups and skip the initial moisture-removal step

ingredients.name: sweetened condensed milk
visual_checks: about half a standard 400 g tin
ingredients.notes: about half a standard 400 g tin

subs.name: 0.75 cup sugar dissolved in 0.5 cup milk, cooked down to a thick paste — takes an extra 10 minutes
subs.notes: add a knob of butter at the end

subs.name: 1 tsp rose water stirred in off heat for a different, floral fragrance

ingredients.name: ghee
visual_checks: for greasing the pan and palms
ingredients.notes: for greasing the pan and palms

steps.text: Grease a heavy-bottomed kadai lightly with the ghee and set over medium heat. Add the freshly grated coconut and stir for 2 minutes to drive off surface moisture — you will hear a faint sizzle. If using desiccated coconut, skip this step.

Pour the condensed milk over the coconut and stir well to combine. The mixture will look very wet initially. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring continuously so the bottom does not catch and scorch, for 10–12 minutes until the mixture thickens noticeably, pulls away from the sides of the pan in a mass, and a line drawn through it with a spoon holds briefly.

Add cardamom powder and stir through. Remove from heat. The mixture will be very sticky at this stage — that is correct. Spread it onto a plate lightly greased with ghee and let it cool for 8–10 minutes until it is comfortable to handle but still warm.

Grease your palms with a little ghee. Scoop about 1.5 tablespoons of the mixture, press firmly into a ball and roll between both palms until smooth. Press a cashew half on top, or roll the ball in chopped pistachios for a green-studded finish. Set on a plate.

Refrigerate the ladoos for 30 minutes to firm them up. They set to a satisfying, slightly chewy texture. Serve at room temperature — straight from the fridge they can be a little firm. They keep for 3–4 days refrigerated (2 days at room temperature if made with fresh coconut, up to a week with desiccated).

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: condensed-milk → 0.75 cup sugar dissolved in 0.5 cup milk, cooked down to a thick paste — takes an extra 10 minutes (add a knob of butter at the end); coconut → desiccated coconut — use 1.5 cups and skip the initial moisture-removal step; cardamom → 1 tsp rose water stirred in off heat for a different, floral fragrance
